g.text: .bss Section Handling: File vs Memory Representation
mem + phdr->p_filesz, // Start after file data

div.p: Purpose: Uninitialized global variables don't need stored values — they just need allocated memory. Benefit: Saves disk space. A 1MB buffer costs 1MB of disk if in .data, but 0 bytes in .bss. The Loader's Job:

ul.li: Zeros the difference (p_memsz - p_filesz) This is why p_memsz > p_filesz for segments containing .bss sections.

div.p: .bss Section ⚠️ No bytes in file! Section header exists in SHT, but sh_offset points to nowhere meaningful. sh_type = SHT_NOBITS
